instructions.original_snippets: NEVER default to the most powerful model for every task — oversized models inflate costs without quality gain on simple tasks.
NEVER pick a model based on benchmark leaderboards alone — benchmark tasks often differ from production workloads.
NEVER hardcode model names in agent workflows — providers rename and deprecate models; use model tier aliases (fast/balanced/reasoning).
Parse task description from $ARGUMENTS, classify against decision matrix, output recommendation using format template.
Model: haiku — this skill itself uses the fast tier because model selection is a classification task, not a reasoning task.
Output format: recommended model + tier label + one-line rationale + estimated cost tier (low/medium/high).
